---
output: github_document
---



```{r, echo = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-"
)
```

# bibliogR <img src="man/figures/logo.svg" align="right" width="120" />

Searching and Citing the Literature.



## Overview




## Installation

Before you can install *teachR* itself, you will also need to install from CRAN the following R packages:  

```{r, eval = FALSE}
install.packages("knitr")
```

Then, install *teachR* from its GitHub public repository:  

```{r, eval = FALSE}
devtools::install.github("NicolasJBM/teachR")
```



## Usage

Administer tests: generation, grading, evaluation, and feedback.

Build question banks: create, test, review, and publish questions; change and update structures


## Toolboxes
